content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 19, 2006

Ron Prentice
Executive Director
California Family Council
P.O. Box 20012
Riverside, CA 92516-0012

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-116

Dear Mr. Prentice:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We are in receipt of your letter dated May 15, 2006. Unfortunately, the letter was not received by this office until June 12, 2006, after the date of the June election. After consideration of your request, we have decided to decline to provide you written advice on your question. The Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

If you have any questions, feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-116

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: The Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(gXA).)
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific question was not disclosed in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request concerned past conduct related to an election that had already occurred (June election), and the Commission does not advise on past conduct under its regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter without full Question/Conclusion sections. The original question was not included in the document. The file number uses 'W-' prefix (W-06-116) which appears to indicate withdrawn or declination status. Document is dated June 19, 2006.

CONCLUSION: The Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(gXA).)

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to the California Family Council regarding a campaign provisions question because the request was received after the June election date and the Commission does not advise on past conduct.
